title: Level 9.1
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 9.1: "Away from Home"}}
{{Class Ψ|security=Stable|safety=Safe}} '''Level 9.1''' is a respective sub-level between '''[[Level 9|The Darkened Suburbs]]''' and '''[[Level 10|The Bumper Crop]]'''. == Description == '''Level 9.1''', otherwise known as '''Away From Home,''' is a presumably infinite level composed of endless snowy roads, neighborhoods, and collections of residential buildings at dusk all bathed in neon lights. Such buildings can include houses, apartments, hotels, inns, hostels and motels; though it should be noted that the buildings never seem to exceed three floors in height nor be overtly posh, instead maintaining an environment resembling a suburban area or the outskirts of a city. {{Blurimage|file=9.1_new.png|caption=A pink street.|align=Right}} The interiors of each building seem normal enough, though they are decorated in a strange and alienating way- everything is clean and orderly, and there is a complete absence of any personal items or photos of humans. Exact layouts can vary, but the suburban homes typically have 2 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms. The layout of the urban buildings is much more varied and hard to predict. The general structure of the level is somewhat more resistant to damage than one would expect, but not completely immune, meaning parts of the level could theoretically be repurposed, though this is unlikely to happen (see '''Wanderlust Effect'''). '''Level 9.1''' has functional amenities such as running water and electricity throughout, though the internet connection is quite poor. Despite the potential abundance of living space, a significant portion of '''Level 9.1''' 's buildings and rooms are permanently locked. However, the ones that are unlocked have a softaid for most other levels. The provided consumables are always packaged and room temperature (e.g. chips, bottled water, [[Object 1|Almond Water]], etc.) and as such have little risk of spoiling and can be easily carried from one location to another. Additionally, the resources found in '''Level 9.1''''s buildings are always safe to consume- bottles of [[Object 44|Cashew Water]] do not appear, for example. Appliances found in the kitchens of suburban homes are functional, but have little use given the lack of raw ingredients or foods that require preparation to consume. Electronics are not quite as useful here due to the spotty internet connection throughout the level, and although modern devices like TVs and computers are fairly common within the buildings, they will not do one much good. {{Blurimage|file=9.1_2.png|caption=The neighborhood.}} The streets of '''Level 9.1''' can vary greatly depending on the area. In the sections resembling suburban neighborhoods, they are usually wider and emptier; houses have yards, and there is generally more space to explore. In sections that contain buildings like hotels and inns, however, the streets are much narrower. Despite the many signs advertising vacancies and cheap rooms in these areas, it is much less likely to find an unlocked door here than in one of the neighborhoods. Cars of various makes and models can frequently be found in the alleyways of downtown areas, but seem to always be locked. While the level seems to be eternally stuck in a state of dawn or dusk (the specific time is hard to determine due to the lack of any celestial bodies), the neon lights scattered around are typically sufficient to light one's path while traveling within '''Level 9.1'''. Such light sources seem to usually be colored in shades of pink or blue, even including lights that would normally be another color, like vehicle headlights, ceiling lights and street lamps. In addition to '''Level 9.1''''s unchanging sky, the weather in this level is also stagnant. There are never clouds, and it never rains or snows within the level, despite the layer of snow already on the ground- even wind seems to be absent, with the air being unusually still. The structure of the level does not appear to account for precipitation, either; the houses do not have gutters, and the streets do not have storm drains to direct rainwater. The only sounds to be heard within this level are your own footsteps and the faint hum of the neon lights. The temperature of this level is always 30 degrees Fahrenheit. Unlike some other levels, space within '''Level 9.1''' is completely linear, meaning it is possible to map, though wanderers who enter always seem to appear at different locations, so converging with others is difficult. On a larger scale, roads tend to form rectangular grid patterns for the most part, and the landscape does not seem to vary in elevation beyond 10 meters, so there are no valleys or mountains- just buildings stretching as far as the eye can see. == Entities == '''Level 9.1''' has no native life. Including being completely devoid of all entities, even peaceful ones such as [[Entity 9|Facelings]], no organic matter of any kind has been found to naturally occur within this level. Even rare instances of plant life such as grass, bushes and trees are actually made of some kind of synthetic material resembling plastic, however realistic they may seem. == "Wanderlust" Effect == A unique psychological effect has been repeatedly observed in wanderers within '''Level 9.1''', nicknamed the "Wanderlust Effect". It manifests solely, without fail, as the desire to exit '''Level 9.1'''. The term was officially coined after multiple organizations made attempts to colonize the level, but all members eventually dispersed in less than a week. Immediately upon entering the level, wanderers will want to leave, despite there being no visible or recorded danger. There does not appear to be any chemical agent or natural environmental factors contributing to this effect, since '''Level 9.1''' is perfectly safe, though it has been theorized that the level's sheer absence of life even on a microbial scale may incite a subconscious feeling of discomfort. Strangely, upon inquiry, neither wanderers currently within the level nor those who had left it could seem to accurately describe the sensation, with responses varying between dissatisfaction, discomfort, melancholy and nostalgia. The longer one resides in the level, the stronger the feeling becomes. One wanderer who volunteered to be restrained had to be freed and escorted out of the level after her repeated escape attempts began to damage her own body. She lasted approximately 5 days. Extensive testing by '''[[M.E.G.]]''' personnel has revealed that the '''Wanderlust Effect''' appears to influence all creatures and entities that enter '''Level 9.1''', not just humans, which is likely why it is devoid of life despite being so peaceful. There is currently no method to completely negate the effect, though consuming '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''' seems to temporarily dull it. == Colonies & Outposts == Despite having ideal conditions for habitation and sufficient resources, the '''Wanderlust Effect''' has rendered permanent colonization of '''Level 9.1''' impossible- many organizations have tried and failed. However, '''Level 9.1''' remains a safe level to travel through, so it is commonly used as a landmark or pit stop of sorts for wanderers. == Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === * Entering a certain house near a pink or blue colored streetlight in '''[[Level 9]]''' leads here; * Rarely, wandering far enough in '''[[Level 18]]''' leads here. === Exits === * Descending into a subway entrance somewhere in the downtown area will lead to '''[[Level 154]].''' * Finding a ladder into the ceiling of a suburban house leads to the [[The Attic|'''Attic''']]. {{Redirect|Prev=9.01|Next=9.2}} {{Collapse|initial='''> Author and Editor Information'''|final='''> Close Author and Editor Information'''|body='''Original Author''': [[User:SlenderTales|SlenderTales]]
Both photos are unedited from a unique phenomenon in Birmingham, UK, in which the sky turned pink.{{User:SlenderTales/MyPages}} }} __NOTOC__ __INDEX__ __NOEDITSECTION__ [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Sub-Levels]] [[Category:Vaporwave]] [[Category:Mental Hazards]] [[zh:Level 9.1]]
